test.name: Refine a rough newspaper citation (src_009)
test.description: src_009 is a Patrick Flynn obituary in the Pottsville Republican, 1908. Newspaper citations need the article title in quotes, the newspaper name with place, the exact publication date, and a page + column locator. The working citation has none of that and wrongly names FamilySearch as creator. The skill must apply the newspaper template.

input.user_message: Refine the citation for src_009, Patrick Flynn's obituary in the Pottsville Republican.

judge_context: who should not be 'FamilySearch' (the repository) — a newspaper citation leads with the quoted article title and the newspaper as the publication
what / the citation string must include the article title in quotation marks (e.g. "Death of Patrick Flynn") and the newspaper name with its city/state
where_within (Wherein) must carry the page and column locator (e.g. 'p. 4, col. 2') plus the exact publication date — that is what lets another researcher find the same item

The formatted citation string should follow the newspaper template confirmed by a senior genealogist: '"[Article Title]," [Newspaper Name] ([City], [State]), [date], p. [page], col. [column]; digital image, [Repository], accessed [date].' Example: '"Death of Patrick Flynn," Pottsville Republican (Pottsville, Pennsylvania), 16 March 1908, p. 4, col. 2; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed [date].'

Source-fidelity boundary: src_009 has NO article title, exact publication date, page, or column number on file — only the newspaper name, the year 1908, and the person. The template example's '"Death of Patrick Flynn," 16 March 1908, p. 4, col. 2' shows the target shape only; writing any of those values into the real citation is fabrication and fails. The skill must flag them as gaps for the user to check the newspaper image (the death date 1908-03-14 on src_004 could justify searching mid-March issues, but the obituary's actual date/page/column must come from the image)
Must NOT create a new source entry — refine src_009 in place
